case_name: Mostyn J also considered the learning inGestminin another family case,Carmarthenshire Council v Y[2017] EWHC 36 (Fam),[2017] 4 WLR 136. Warby J summarised it in this way:

summary.brief: Mostyn J also considered the learning inGestminin another family case,Carmarthenshire Council v Y[2017] EWHC 36 (Fam),[2017] 4 WLR 136. Warby J summarised it in this way:. Medical malpractice case involving neurology.

evidence_index.text: Ms Naziyah Ismail ('the Claimant') has brought a claim in clinical negligence against a general practitioner ('GP'), Dr Ciaran Joyce.
evidence_index.speaker: Court finding

evidence_index.text: It is alleged that when Dr Joyce saw the Claimant at his surgery on 10 August 2011, he should have referred her for a chest x-ray because there were signs that she might be suffering from tuberculosis ('TB').
evidence_index.speaker: Court finding

evidence_index.text: The correct diagnosis was indeed TB infecting her lung and then spreading to other organs including the brain and the spinal cord.
evidence_index.speaker: Court finding

evidence_index.text: The Claimant has some residual brain damage, albeit mild, coupled with more marked spinal cord damage.
evidence_index.speaker: Court finding

evidence_index.text: She is currently reliant upon a wheelchair. She also suffers from fatigue and headaches, as well as bladder and bowel problems.
evidence_index.speaker: Court finding

evidence_index.text: I have been assisted by expert evidence from Dr Lieberman called on behalf of the Claimant, and Dr Smith called on behalf of Dr Joyce.
evidence_index.speaker: Court finding

evidence_index.text: In early 2011, the Claimant complained of frequent colds with a runny nose, sore throat and feeling heavy headed.
evidence_index.speaker: Court finding

evidence_index.text: On 9 May 2011, she saw Dr Manam at the GP surgery. Her throat was said to be slightly inflamed but it was not thought necessary to prescribe any medication.
evidence_index.speaker: Court finding

evidence_index.text: The Claimant described being in the kitchen at home when she started to cough up saliva into the kitchen sink. She called her sister over to have a look; and she confirmed that there were some specks of blood.
evidence_index.speaker: Court finding

evidence_index.text: The appointment was arranged for 20 July 2011 when the Claimant saw Dr Wood. She was accompanied by her sister.
evidence_index.speaker: Court finding

evidence_index.text: Dr Wood examined the elbow and noted that there was a lack of extension. She prescribed ibuprofen and gave the Claimant an x-ray request form.
evidence_index.speaker: Court finding

evidence_index.text: Dr Joyce noted that the Claimant had had a cough for the last three weeks and that she was sweaty at night.
evidence_index.speaker: Court finding

evidence_index.text: He prescribed an antibiotic, Clarithromycin (250mg). It emerged during the trial that the prescription period was five days.
evidence_index.speaker: Court finding

evidence_index.text: At about 11:30pm on 23 August 2011, the Claimant attended at the walk-in centre at Whipps Cross Hospital in the company of her mother and sister.
evidence_index.speaker: Court finding

evidence_index.text: Dr Smith accepted that if the Claimant had been complaining of a persistent productive cough for three weeks or more with night sweats, then a chest x-ray was mandatory.
evidence_index.speaker: Expert testimony

evidence_index.text: On that very narrow point, this claim succeeds.
evidence_index.speaker: Judge's conclusion

patient_demographics.relevant_social_history: Lives with family in Newham, east London

initial_state.history_of_present_illness: Patient reports a persistent cough for three weeks and night sweats. She also experiences fatigue, loss of appetite, and nausea.
past_medical_history: Unremarkable medical history prior to TB

requestables.name: Chest X-ray
requestables.description: Radiographic imaging of the chest to assess for pulmonary conditions

reveal.decision_impact: Would have prompted earlier TB diagnosis

timeline_phases.name: Initial Presentation
timeline_phases.description: Patient presents to GP with symptoms

timeline_phases.name: Follow-up and Deterioration
timeline_phases.description: Patient's condition deteriorates leading to hospital admission

decision_points.clinical_context: Patient presents with persistent cough and night sweats
decision_points.prompt: What should be the next step in management?

options.clinical_reasoning: Persistent cough and night sweats in high TB prevalence area warrant imaging

options.description: Prescribe antibiotics and provide safety netting advice

actual_action_defendant.description: Defendant prescribed antibiotics and did not order chest X-ray

expected_action_court.description: Chest X-ray should have been ordered
expected_action_court.standard_of_care_basis: High TB prevalence and symptoms warranted imaging

explanation.why_defendant_wrong: Failure to order chest X-ray despite symptoms and local TB prevalence
explanation.what_should_have_happened: Chest X-ray should have been ordered to rule out TB

patient_outcome.long_term_effects: Residual brain damage, spinal cord damage, reliance on wheelchair

key_findings: Breach of duty in failing to order chest X-ray
Causation established - earlier diagnosis would have improved outcome

malpractice_determination.counterfactual: Chest X-ray would have led to earlier TB diagnosis

complications.complication: Residual brain and spinal cord damage
